description: §19.9.3 body-split — a server-fn call as a NON-top-level statement (inside an if arm). refresh() is a client handler; @items = loadTasks() sits inside `if (@mode == "all")`, so the server call cannot be a bare top-level await — it compiles to an awaited continuation INSIDE the branch. A #refresh click takes the @mode == "all" arm; after settle the continuation runs and the three rows hydrate (.row count 3, #count 3). Exercises the body-split + settle drain through control flow.
rationale: §19.9.3 / §12 a server-fn call interleaved with client control flow is split across the boundary; the client continuation (the reactive @items write) runs after the server route resolves. The if-guard is preserved and the hydrate completes post-settle even though the call is non-top-level.
